layers.title: Record sampler design
layers.summary: The repository context now routes logging-sampler questions to a dedicated design document. That document defines the sampling categories, hot-path constraints, and compatibility stance that the later implementation follows.
layers.diff:
+| design unified class-based random log sampling | `modules/infra/logging/log-sampler-design.md` | `src/datasystem/common/log/*`, `src/datasystem/common/log/spdlog/*`, `src/datasystem/common/metrics/hard_disk_exporter/*`, `src/datasystem/common/util/gflag/*` |
+- `LogSampler` provides unified random hash-threshold sampling across request/access/diagnostic dimensions, with per-request granularity and low-overhead hot-path decisions.
+- Provide `log_rate_limit` compatibility. The old field is removed and not mapped into the new sampling rates.
+| `share_memory.proto` | Add `LogSampleConfigPb`; `RegisterClientRspPb reserved 24` and `log_sample_config = 25`; `HeartbeatRspPb.log_sample_config = 8` |

layers.title: Rename deployment knobs
layers.summary: Deployment and operator docs switch from the single per-second `log_rate_limit` knob to three decimal sample-rate knobs. Helm, dscli, worker config, and log/client environment guides are updated to document request, access, and diagnostic sampling behavior.

bcFindings.evidence: Deployment docs and worker config remove `log_rate_limit` and replace it with `request_sample_rate`, `access_sample_rate`, and `diagnostic_sample_rate`; existing startup/config automation using the old flag has no compatibility mapping.
`docs/source_zh_cn/appendix/client_env_guide.md` removes the client environment variable entry, and `Logging::InitClientAdvancedConfig` no longer reads `LOG_RATE_LIMIT_ENV`.

layers.title: Carry sampler config
layers.summary: The wire schema adds `LogSampleConfigPb` and sends it in register and heartbeat responses. This is the schema foundation used by clients to receive the worker-authoritative sampling configuration.

layers.title: Replace rate limiter
layers.summary: The old first-N `LogRateLimiter` is deleted and replaced with a process singleton `LogSampler` using precomputed ppm thresholds and an atomic snapshot. Runtime log creation now asks the sampler before stream payload construction, while FATAL and background cases are preserved.

layers.title: Expose sample-rate API
layers.summary: The embedded-worker public configuration API moves from one integer rate limit to three validated decimal sampling rates. The gflag layer mirrors that shape and validates dynamic updates as a sampler-specific transaction.

layers.title: Propagate sampling decisions
layers.summary: Worker register and heartbeat responses now populate `LogSampleConfigPb`, and clients apply that config when it is present. ZMQ trace metadata switches from rate-limiter decisions to sampler state so request sampling remains consistent across RPC hops.

layers.title: Add access facades
layers.summary: AccessRecorder gains typed Object, Stream, and RequestOut facades that lazily materialize fields only when sampling says to record. This layer is the API used by the following call-site cohort to avoid constructing request metadata on sampled-out paths.

bcFindings.evidence: `src/datasystem/protos/share_memory.proto` changes field 24 from `int32 log_rate_limit` to `LogSampleConfigPb log_sample_config`, so old clients expecting the int32 field cannot consume the same public response contract.
`src/datasystem/common/log/spdlog/log_rate_limiter.h` and `.cpp` are deleted, and build targets remove the `log_rate_limiter` library.

The old `AccessRecorder::Record(..., std::string dataSize, RequestParam, ...)` style is removed in favor of typed facades and uint64/provider-based data size handling, changing callers that used the previous public method shape.

layers.title: Migrate SDK wrappers
layers.summary: C, C++, Java, and Python client paths are moved from eager `RequestParam` assembly to the typed access recorder facade. The behavior remains an access-log instrumentation change, but the new shape delays expensive key and data-size formatting until sampling admits the record.

layers.title: Migrate worker access logs
layers.summary: Worker object-cache and stream-cache service handlers switch to the same lazy facade. This extends sampling savings to foreground service paths while preserving result recording and operation-specific fields.

layers.title: Adjust infra call sites
layers.summary: ETCD/request-out and metrics-adjacent code is updated to compile with the new access recorder and exporter dependencies. These are supporting migrations so the recorder API change is applied consistently across internal infrastructure paths.

layers.title: Replace limiter tests
layers.summary: Unit tests remove the old rate-limiter suite and add coverage for sampler config, access sampling, integration with log macros, and typed access facades. Existing logging and ZMQ trace tests are adapted to the sampler semantics.

layers.title: Cover cross-process config
layers.summary: A system test is added for worker-to-client sampler config delivery and request sampling consistency across processes. The existing one-worker log-size scenario drops the old unlimited `log_rate_limit=0` flag because pass-through is now the default.

layers.title: Refresh performance guardrails
layers.summary: The performance test changes from first-N rate-limit assertions to random sampler throughput, p99, payload-build, and access-recorder sampled-out checks. These tests target the hot-path contract introduced in the sampler design.

summary: This PR replaces the old per-second log rate limiter with a worker-authoritative random LogSampler, propagates sampling config to clients, rewrites access logging to lazy typed facades, updates deployment docs/config, and adds sampler-focused tests.
